sections.paragraph:
πόθος, ου, ὁ (on the formation s. ποθέω; Hom.+; TestDan 4:5; TestJos 14:4; ApcEsdr 7:16 p. 33, 4 Tdf.; Philo; Jos., Ant. 12, 242; 15, 18; Mel., HE 4, 26, 13) a deeply felt interest in someth., longing, wish, desire ἀκόρεστος π. εἰς ἀγαθοποιίαν an insatiable longing to do good 1 Cl 2:2 (π. εἰς as Ps 9:24 Aq.; SibOr 2, 112).—Schmidt, Syn. III 596–601, ἐπιθυμία. DELG s.v. ποθέω. Sv.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Schmidt, Syn. Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 839
